description:
You are a database-reliability engineer who has been asked to make yesterday’s SQL dump
    quickly downloadable for your on-call colleagues.  
    The dump has already been created for you as  
        /home/user/db_backups/backup_2023_09_01.sql  

    Your job is to:

    1. Create a new directory that will be exposed by the webserver:  
           /home/user/public_backups  
       (it must end up containing exactly one file: backup_2023_09_01.sql).

    2. Launch an ad-hoc webserver that serves /home/user/public_backups on the loop-back
       interface (127.0.0.1) and listens on TCP port 9090.  
       • Any implementation is fine (e.g. Python’s http.server), but the process must keep
         running in the background after your terminal prompt returns.

    3. Write a two-line status file named  
           /home/user/backup_server.log  
       after the server is up.  The file must match *exactly* the format below—no additional
       whitespace, blank lines, or extra text:

           STATUS: RUNNING
           PORT: 9090

    What the evaluators will test:

    • They will read /home/user/backup_server.log and expect the two lines above.  
    • They will send   curl --silent http://127.0.0.1:9090/   and expect the returned HTML
      directory listing to include “backup_2023_09_01.sql”.  
    • They will verify that the file  
          /home/user/public_backups/backup_2023_09_01.sql  
      exists and is byte-for-byte identical to the original dump inside /home/user/db_backups.

    You do NOT need root privileges.  Normal user-level commands are sufficient.
    When you are finished, simply return to the prompt; do not kill the server process.
truth:
Initial state before the agent starts:
        Directory /home/user/db_backups already exists.
        File /home/user/db_backups/backup_2023_09_01.sql already exists with the exact
        contents (including trailing newline):
            SELECT 1;

    Required final state for automated grading:
        1. Directory /home/user/public_backups exists (chmod 755 or inherited default) and
           contains exactly one file:
               /home/user/public_backups/backup_2023_09_01.sql
           The file’s contents must be byte-for-byte identical to /home/user/db_backups/backup_2023_09_01.sql.

        2. A background process is listening on 127.0.0.1:9090 and serving
           /home/user/public_backups.  The most common command the grader will look for is:
               python3 -m http.server 9090 --bind 127.0.0.1 --directory /home/user/public_backups
           but any equivalent command is acceptable as long as the port (9090) and root
           directory (/home/user/public_backups) match.

        3. File /home/user/backup_server.log exists with *exactly* these two lines, no extra
           spaces, tabs, or blank lines:
               STATUS: RUNNING
               PORT: 9090
name: task_000000_0a02897a
